title: Chloroleucon tenuiflorum
taxonomy: Chloroleucon tenuiflorum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or legume family. It belongs to the genus Chloroleucon and is native to tropical Africa. This plant is a type of tree or shrub that grows in various habitats, including tropical forests and savannas. Chloroleucon tenuiflorum is often used as a medicinal plant due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's classification is further divided into different species and subspecies based on its morphological characteristics.
